danger_signals.visual_form: Bright contrasting bullets/energy bolts moving toward player
Distinct alien/mechanical designs in screen space
Enemies or hazards filling lateral or vertical screen space

safety_signals.visual_form: Empty black/dark background with no hostile sprites
Distinct collectible items with bright/contrasting color
Continuous player projectile stream visible on screen

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Variety of hostile spacecraft with non-human morphology
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Enemies spawned from top/sides of screen; alien mechanical design language

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dense enemy sprite density on screen; frequent projectile exchanges
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies pursue player; continuous fire patterns

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Screen clears of hostile entities; brief visual stillness
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Reduced enemy activity or spawning pause

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Larger or more varied enemy types appearing; increased projectile density
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy attack patterns become more complex; movement speed increases

environmental_storytelling.example: Successive waves introduce increasingly sophisticated alien spacecraft, suggesting escalating invasion force
Larger, visually dominant enemies signal boss encounters or major threats vs. smaller grunt units
Bullet pattern richness and coordination between enemy types implies advancing alien tactical capability

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Moves in formation or patrol pattern; fires toward player position
Larger hitbox; complex multi-phase attack patterns; sustained on screen longer than standard enemies
Responds to input; maintains weapon fire stream; can move freely within screen bounds
Travels upward; removes enemy on contact
Travels toward player; removes player on contact or triggers evasion

notes: Super E.D.F. is a vertically-scrolling shoot-em-up with straightforward danger/safety binary communication. World-state is communicated primarily through enemy density, variety, and attack complexity rather than environmental backdrop changes. The game uses classic shmup visual language: bright contrasting sprites on dark background, projectile-based threat signaling, and resource management through power-ups. Political/faction dimension is minimal—primarily humans (E.D.F.) vs. alien invaders. No complex environmental storytelling; communication is purely mechanical/sprite-based.
